top_categories.workout_context: 84ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At Bowral Classic, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Bowral Classic. Train it until it feels boring.
At mile 60 of 94, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Bowral Classic's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before October.
7,851ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
Bowral Classic's paved rolling hills through Southern Highlands forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 94 miles.
7,851ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 94 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 7,851ft of climbing on paved rolling hills through Southern Highlands does to your power file on race day.
Tempo is Bowral Classic's race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 94 miles on paved rolling hills through Southern Highlands. Train it until it's automatic.
Low-cadence force work for 7,851ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Bowral Classic’s 94 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on TT Threshold, Over Under, and G Spot to prepare you for 94 miles of paved rolling hills through southern highlands countryside in Bowral, New South Wales, Australia.
